entry.title: Four Clean Core Use Cases for SAP S/4HANA Cloud with SAP Business Technology Platform
entry.content:
Introduction

Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) systems have long been adapted to match companies' distinct business processes. However, as customizations accumulate, organizations are finding that maintaining their ERP functions becomes more complex, often slowing down innovation.  

In this short blog post, I’ll cover four use case examples with SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) you can adopt to simplify ERP upgrades with BTP extensions, while reducing testing time and effort. Customer examples are provided for each scenario, as well as links to technical implementation steps.

  1. Extend SAP S/4HANA processes without impacting core functionality
  2. Connect employees and partners to delight your customers
  3. Simplify process connectivity to any application
  4. Enhance SAP S/4HANA with planning and analysis

About the author

Terry Penner is part of the SAP BTP product marketing team, focused on BTP for SAP S/4HANA. He has more than 20 years of technical and business experience in the SAP platform and analytics, including working directly on hundreds of customer implementations.

Use case #1: Extend SAP S/4HANA processes without impacting core functionality

 

Innovative companies need to give their teams the tools to quickly turn great ideas into real-life changes. But for most businesses, it's still challenging to allow business users to create applications efficiently while maintaining IT governance standards.

Use SAP Build Apps so your SAP S/4HANA users can create extensions quickly without impacting core functionality.  Business users want to create specific workflows quickly without writing code, all while following IT company standards. For example, your team can design mobile and desktop applications and speed up development with prebuilt connectors and business content for SAP and third-party systems.

Freudenberg Group  adopted a low-code development strategy to 200 business users to help the company more quickly digitalize processes. This approach replaced manual paper document exchange, boosting new technology adoption fivefold. Now, the company is expanding this initiative to build a community inside Freudenberg for digital builder to solve key challenges and drive sustainable, value-driven growth.

>> Learn how to implement this use case

 

Use case #2: Connect employees and partners to delight your customers

The Lead to Cash process crosses multiple applications and connects employees and partners from initial interactions to order fulfillment. Real-time inventory levels and locations are vital, ensuring both sales reps and distribution partners have current data, especially when they may be competing for the same inventory.

Use pre-built integrations from SAP Integration Suite (part of SAP BTP) to connect SAP S/4HANA, finance and supply chain systems. As a result, your employees can boost business efficiency and provide a superior customer experience – all without requiring additional IT resources.

Vorwerk transformed the person-to-person sales experience by digitalizing the direct selling process. The world’s largest direct seller of premium household appliances used SAP Integration Suite and SAP BTP to connect back-end systems from SAP and third parties. This greatly reduced sales paperwork and significantly accelerated the sales cycle for their advisors.

>> Learn how to implement this use case

 

Use case #3: Simplify process connectivity to any application

Running a business on siloed legacy systems is no longer a competitive option.  A connected landscape is required to meet employee and customer expectations for simplified mobile access, omnichannel interactions, and collaborative engagement.

Open Connectors, a core component of SAP Integration Suite, offers over 160 prebuilt, feature-rich connectors to help ensure smooth connections with third-party SAP applications. The component helps create an integrated API layer and supports standards-based implementation for SAP and third-party applications, regardless of the back end. It facilitates critical digital capabilities, including mass-updating asset master data, generating purchase requisitions originating from a spreadsheet, and uploading manual entries automatically through an API.

Elkjøp is a prime example of a company leveraging this use case to build integrations with third-party applications. Using SAP Integration Suite, the consumer electronics retailer created an event driven architecture to connect services, repairs, installations, capacity handling and support tickets. The company processes 5 million messages daily to provide an experience where a customer can place an online order and pick it up in-store within 30 minutes.

>> Learn how to implement this use case

 

Use case #4: Enhance SAP S/4HANA with planning and analysis

Effective use of financial data safeguards your business from damaging risks, such as maverick spending, regulatory noncompliance, and reporting errors. But it also optimizes a finance organization's already limited time and energy.

By enabling native connectivity to SAP S/4HANA Cloud data from SAP Analytics Cloud, you can create a fully integrated foundation for aligning your operations and finance strategies. Business leaders can use financial planning and analysis to gain a real-time, business-wide understanding of critical business drivers to mitigate potential risks early, address planning variances proactively, and improve profitability. Company specific financial planning can be accomplished while still allowing for easier updating of SAP S/4HANA core modules.

Take Watco, a leading transportation and logistics provider in the United States. Watco brought together data from more than 200 different profit centers to provide a consolidated company view of financials. This allows the company to analyze different data sets to provide improved forecasting and a better link between financial planning and ERP. 

>> Learn how to implement this use case

 

Innovation without compromising ERP integrity

With SAP BTP together with SAP S/4HANA, it is possible to have the ERP experience that meets your business needs today, tomorrow, and in the future.  By applying these four use cases, you can help ensure the ERP system operates efficiently and supports the innovation of digital capabilities unique to your business. You can design custom applications, create connected experiences, simplify connectivity, and align operations and finances – all without compromising the integrity of your ERP investment.

For more use cases that can unleash your company’s potential, learn more about SAP Business Technology Platform with SAP S/4HANA.

 

Explore more related content in this series about SAP BTP with S/4HANA:

Overview

  • 2024 SAP BTP Year in Review: Release Highlights for SAP S/4HANA users

Artificial Intelligence

  • Artificial Intelligence (AI) for SAP S/4HANA and SAP BTP: A deep dive
  • How SAP Business AI and Large Language Models are shaping the future of SAP S/4HANA and SAP BTP 

Data Migration

  • SAP S/4HANA Data Migration and Master Data Management Best Practices with SAP BTP

Extensions

  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Extensions with SAP Build Best Practices: An Expert Roundtable
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Extensions and Clean Core with SAP BTP - A Best Practices Conversation

entry.title: How to migrate data from SAP BW to SAP Datasphere BW bridge or SAP BW/4HANA using the Expert View
entry.content:
Abstract

If you are on BW 7.x, planning to modernize your SAP BW system with SAP Datasphere BW bridge or SAP BW/4HANA (on-prem or PCE), and want to save the investments made along the history, you should consider a path which can help you to migrate your data in an automated way, without the need of reinventing the wheel or having additional manual efforts such as data reloads or reinitializations .

This can be achieved with the Remote Conversion, please see my previous blogs on the topic and the Conversion Guide:

1. SAP Datasphere, SAP BW bridge: Demystifying the Remote Conversion

2. SAP Datasphere, BW bridge: How to transfer data from SAP BW or BW/4HANA to a 2-tier tenant landscape

3. SAP Datasphere, SAP BW bridge - Remote Conversion Guide

This blog intends to provide you additional details on how to optimize the data migration by using the 'Expert View' of the Remote Conversion. This capability is available for both SAP Datasphere BW bridge and SAP BW/4HANA and, proven by project experience, it can improve the performance and make your migration runtime up to 20x faster in comparison with the 'Standard View'.

Figure 1: Data migration options

Motivation

When using the Remote Conversion, one (1) program is generated for every task type of a table in scope. See the Conversion Guide section 3.4 Generation Phase for further details.

Those programs are executed by the tool considering the following sequence:

  1. Read: data selection in the sender system. These programs run in the sender system.
  2. Move: data migration from the sender system to the receiver system. These programs run in the sender system and trigger DIA jobs in the receiver system.
  3. Conversion: data insertion into the corresponding tables of the receiver system. These programs run in the receiver system.

There are other types of programs such as DELTA_MERGE and MOVE_TO_ADSO which run in the receiver system, but not relevant for the purpose of this explanation.

Figure 2: Data transfer logic and sequence

This is the standard behaviour of the tool. This way, each table will have the corresponding phases processed sequentially. 

When you get to the Migration Phase in the Conversion Cockpit, there are basically 2 ways of running the data migration. 

1. “Start Migration” option

It uses the "Standard View" and "Extended View". With this option, the tool automatically controls the sequence/scheduling of the data migration tasks for each table (read –> move –> conversion).

Although it follows a logical sequence, this option may create some idle time in both sender and receiver system, reason why you see the systems not running in full capacity at certain times. It may also create delays for the completeness of the tasks in the sender system - usually the critical path as the sender system is live/operational - making its release to take longer.

Figure 3: Start Migration

2. “Start Transformation of Identified Tables (Optional)" option

It uses the "Expert View". With this option, it is possible to define/control the sequence and the number of jobs for table types, critical tables, etc. , by for example focusing on the read/move tasks in the sender system. This way, it's possible to finish the sender system tasks faster, release the system and then focus on the receiver tasks afterwards. As the receiver system is usually not operational yet, this approach is commonly agreed with customers. 

This option is not automated like the “Start Migration” one, it requires manual work/intervention and some experience with the data migration logic. This manual intervention is needed to optimize the sequences and keep the systems as busy as possible. By avoiding the idle times, it's possible to considerably improve the overall migration runtimes.

Figure 4: Start Transformation of Identified Tables (Optional)

The Conversion Guide section 3.6 Migration Phase describes the option 1.

I will show you in this blog how to use the option 2.

Execution

1. Add the parameter 'PCL_EXPERT' = "X" to the user (s) who will execute the migration package. This will enable the 'Expert View" option in Process Monitor of the Conversion Cockpit.

Figure 5: Parameters in SU01

2. Check the table CNVLTRL_TEC in the sender system. The field TABGROUP contains the sequence of the table types to be executed.

Figure 6: Table CNVLTRL_TEC - TABGROUP 09

In general there are 6 TABGROUP values:

  • TABGROUP 09: T* and some RS* tables 
  • TABGROUP 10: some SID tables such as /BI0/SCALWEEK, /BI0/SCALYEAR, /BI0/SCURRENCY, etc.
  • TABGROUP 11: /BI0/SCALQUARTER and /BI0/SFISCYEAR
  • TABGROUP 12: /BI0/SCALMONTH and /BI0/SFISCPER
  • TABGROUP 13: /BI0/SDATE
  • TABGROUP 30: all others

In the example I will be demonstrating, there are only tables under TABGROUP 09 and TABGROUP 30, and the content of each TABGROUP may slightly differ depending on the scope that was collected.

3. Run “Start Transformation of Identified Tables (Optional)" as indicated in the Figure 4.

4. Select "Start tasks in sender system", "Read Tasks" and "Move Cluster Tasks", and start the execution with the tables under TABGROUP 09.

Figure 7: Start Transformation of Identified Tables (Optional) - TABGROUP 09

The flag "Per Table in Sender System" will ensure each selected table will have at least 1 job assigned. The number of jobs to be used depends on how many jobs (BGD work processes) are available in the system. Check the transaction SM50 to verify this information. It's possible to modify the number or DIA and BDG to optimize the execution (transaction RZ03).

5. Run "Monitor Transformation Status for Active Package (Expert)" to display/monitor the tables. You may consider to open this screen in a new session and keep it open for continuous/faster monitoring. 

Figure 8: Conversion Monitor - TABGROUP 09

If you filter the monitor including the "Jobs To Do', you can also display the tables that did not start yet.

Figure 9: Conversion Monitor for tables with status "To Do"

6. Repeat step (4), now for the tables under TABGROUP 30 (or TABGROUP 10 and every subsequent group in case your scope contains tables under those groups).

You should get the below pop up in case you did close the previous session and need to run it again. Select 'Yes" and proceed.

Figure 10: Confirm the start of a new execution for tables

Figure 11: Table CNVLTRL_TEC - TABGROUP 30

It's possible to select the Application Server that will process the set of tables (relevant in case there is more than one Application Server available).

Figure 12: Start Transformation of Identified Tables (Optional) - TABGROUP 30

Figure 13: Conversion Monitor - TABGROUP 30

Let's have a look on the status of some tables, sorted by Task Type:

Figure 14: Conversion Monitor - status per Task Type

You can also use the "Quick Select" button to select and release tables by type. By having this distribution, you can increase the level of parallelization and ensure as many tables as possible to run. I am now selecting all SID tables, only the ones not yet started will be triggered.

Figure 15: Start Transformation of Identified Tables (Optional) - Quick Select

Here we can see that all SID tables were finished:

Figure 16: Conversion Monitor - SID tables

I will release 8 new jobs, without specifying any table or type. This way, the tool will randomly pick up tables which did not start yet.

Figure 17: Conversion Monitor - new tables being picked

Updating the monitor, all tables have finished the Read and Move tasks:

Figure 18: Conversion Monitor - Read and Move tasks are finished

In "Monitor: Transformation Status (Optional)", we can confirm the Read and Move tasks were finished for all tables in scope. 

Figure 19: Monitor: Transformation Status (Optional)

With the Read and Move phases finished, this means there are no further tasks to run in the sender system. The sender system can be handed over to the customer and the data loads/process chains there can be resumed in the sender system. 

Before handing over the sender system, you should:

  • Make sure the table RSMIGROBJ in the sender system is empty. This is the lock table for the objects in scope.
  • Run "Confirm Data Selection for Participating BW Objects is Finished in Original System" in the Task List and sync with the Conversion Cockpit.

Figure 20: Confirm Data Selection for Participating BW Objects is Finished in Original System

7. Repeat steps (3) to (6), the difference is that now you should select "Start tasks in receiver system".

Figure 21: Conversion Monitor - insertion for TABGROUP 09

Figure 22: Conversion Monitor - insertion for TABGROUP 30

Figure 23: Overall status per phase and last table in progress

With "Verify Task Completion", you can check if all tasks were finished, including the conversion ones. In case there is any task missing, this activity will raise an error and indicate which table/tasks still needs to be processed.

Figure 24: Verify Task Completion

In "Monitor: Transformation Status (Optional)", all tasks for all tables in scope are now finished.

Figure 25: Monitor: Transformation Status (Optional) - all tasks finished

With that, the new data loads/process chains can now be started in the receiver system. Even if the the data loads were earlier resumed in the sender system, the delta queues were cloned and synchronized - ensuring the consistency of the data loads for both sender and receiver systems. This is one of the benefits of the Remote Conversion, offering the possibility of having both systems running in parallel, for example, during the validation/stabilization period.

Conclusion

The scope of this run contained 130 tables (/BI* and RS*) with 100 million records. Up to 8 BTC work processes were used in parallel.

Here a comparison between the runtimes of the 'Standard View" and 'Expert View' for the same scope:

Standard View: 48 minutes

  • Read + Move tasks: 15 minutes
  • Conversion tasks: 33 minutes

Expert View: 16 minutes

  • Read + Move tasks: 5 minutes
  • Conversion tasks: 11 minutes

It was possible to run the data migration 3x faster for this particular scope. The larger the scope, the more this factor can be improved.

For the purpose of this blog, the conversion tasks were triggered only after the read/move ones were finished. In practice, it is also possible to parallelize all task types, allowing even faster runtimes to be achieved.

Felipe Capano

entry.title: SAP CAP Lessons Learned: SQLite is single threaded, HANA is not
entry.content:
Good day fellow CAPpers,

Today I'll share one of the lessons I've learned working with CAP, something that may not be clear in the current tutorials or documentation. When running locally, in a development environment, you usually would choose SQLite. Then on hybrid or production environment you switch to a HANA database.

It happens that you won't be able to simulate the production behavior locally. SQLite is single threaded, and locks the database as one is writing on it.

Suppose you have a code that has 2 functions:

  1. fetch(): Fetches data from an external system and INSERTs in a local table
  2. print(): Prints the last fetched record

And the following data that will be fetched: first, second, third, fourth...

This is what happens if you call three times fetch() then three times print():

  • SQLite: third, third, third
  • HANA*: first, second, third
    • *the actual result may vary according to the time it takes to fetch the data. On the worst case scenario (slowest), it would return: undefined, undefined, undefined 

SQLite will execute database calls in the order they were called to prevent data corruption (even if the calls are in different entities, thus safe), whereas HANA is able to handle concurrent calls.

 

To tell the truth, I'm not sure how's the behavior in Postgres. If you have experience with it, please comment below and share with us.

 

May this post help you on your troubleshooting. Reply if you find any issues so I, and the community, can help.

Lucas Magrini Rigo

entry.title: Navigating SAP's AI Ecosystem: A Strategic Guide for Finance and Procurement Leaders
entry.content:
In today's rapidly evolving business landscape, artificial intelligence has moved beyond being a technological novelty to become a critical driver of operational excellence and competitive advantage. For finance, procurement, and accounting leaders, understanding how to leverage SAP's comprehensive AI portfolio effectively can be the key to unlocking unprecedented efficiency and insights within their organizations.

 

Understanding SAP's AI Portfolio

SAP has structured its AI offerings into three distinct categories, each designed to address different organizational needs and readiness levels:

Base AI represents the foundation of SAP's intelligent enterprise vision. These embedded machine learning features come included with your existing subscription, offering immediate value without additional investment. These capabilities are already woven into your day-to-day operations, enhancing processes from automated invoice matching to predictive analytics for cash flow management.

Premium AI elevates your capabilities with advanced generative AI solutions. These sophisticated tools require AI units—SAP's measurement of computing power consumption—and can be seamlessly integrated into your existing workflows. Think of AI units as the fuel that powers these advanced features, with consumption varying based on the complexity and scale of your use cases across SAP's solution portfolio.

Joule, SAP's AI agent, represents the newest frontier in enterprise AI assistance. Available with a free subscription up to certain usage thresholds, Joule serves as your intelligent business companion, capable of handling complex queries and automating sophisticated workflows across your financial operations.

 

Strategic Implementation: A Practical Approach

To maximize the value of SAP's AI capabilities while ensuring responsible resource allocation, we recommend following a structured approach to implementation:

First and foremost, begin with a clear business need. The most successful AI implementations enhance already robust processes rather than attempting to fix broken ones. Take time to articulate specific challenges within your financial or procurement operations where AI could drive meaningful improvements.

Next, explore SAP's extensive catalog of AI features through the SAP Discovery Center Business AI Features portal (https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/ai-catalog/?category=pp.5). This comprehensive resource allows you to match your business needs with existing capabilities, potentially saving significant time and resources in custom development.

Once you've identified promising features, engage with your Customer Success Manager or Account Executive. These professionals can provide detailed insights into implementation requirements, AI unit needs for Premium features, and ROI calculations tailored to your use case. To better understand the resource requirements, utilize the AI Feature Estimator (https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/ai-estimator) for accurate projections of AI unit consumption and associated costs.

When existing features don't precisely match your needs, consider these alternatives:

1. Joule Integration: Explore how SAP's AI agent can be deployed to address your specific use case, taking advantage of the free subscription tier to pilot the solution.

2. Custom Development: The SAP Business Technology Platform offers a robust foundation for building customized AI solutions. Access the full catalog of AI services through the BTP AI services catalog (https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/servicessearch/AI%20Enabled) and explore existing AI missions (https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/missionCatalog/) that might align with your objectives.

3. Partner Solutions: The SAP Store (https://www.sap.com/store.html) showcases AI solutions developed by certified partners, offering pre-built solutions that might address your needs without requiring custom development.

 

Making the Right Choice

Success in implementing AI within your financial operations depends on careful evaluation of your needs, resources, and objectives. Consider the following factors:

Implementation Timeline: Base AI features offer immediate value with minimal setup, while Premium AI solutions might require more extensive preparation but deliver more sophisticated capabilities.

Resource Requirements: Factor in both the initial investment and ongoing AI unit consumption for Premium AI features. The AI Feature Estimator provides essential insights for accurate cost projections and resource planning.

ROI Potential: Work with your SAP representatives to develop detailed ROI calculations for each potential implementation, ensuring your AI investments align with business objectives.

 

Next Steps

Begin your AI journey by:

1. Documenting specific challenges within your financial or procurement processes

2. Reviewing available features in the SAP Discovery Center

3. Scheduling a consultation with your SAP representative to discuss potential implementations

Remember, the goal is not to implement AI for its own sake but to drive meaningful improvements in your operations. Take advantage of demonstration sessions and pilot programs to ensure each implementation delivers tangible value to your organization.

 

For more information about specific features or to begin your AI implementation journey, visit the SAP Discovery Center's AI catalog or contact your SAP representative. The resources linked throughout this article provide comprehensive guidance for each stage of your AI implementation journey.

entry.title: The 2025 SAP Developer Survey is Live - Contribute Now
entry.content:
Attention SAP Developers!

The 2025 SAP Developer Insights Survey starts today and will be running through the first week in March.  It will take about twelve minutes of your time to complete.

2025 marks the sixth consecutive year for this survey.  If you design, develop, or test software for SAP applications, platforms, or data: we have questions for you.

Take the 2025 SAP Developer Survey now

As always, the survey data in anonymous.  The information we gather is important to us. It is a key resource we use to understand who you are and what you do.  These data play an important role in shaping channels, programs, and to focus new content on your needs.

We also publish the results back to you. If you are curious, have a look at the report describing last year’s survey results.

The survey period begins today, January 23rd. It will continue for six weeks, ending around March 7th. Our report on this 2025 survey will be released a few weeks after that.

Help us help the SAP Community. Your contribution is important.

entry.title: New "Wait for API Call" Integrates Processes with External Systems
entry.content:
SAP Build Process Automation has introduced the "Wait for an API Call" step, that pauses a process instance and waits for some system – any system – to call a specific API to resume the process instance. 

NOTE: The step was scheduled to be introduced this week and next to all the SAP BTP landscapes, but there are no guarantees, and the release could be delayed.

 

Why Use It

I believe the idea behind this is a scenario where, from your process, you call an external system that must execute some task before the rest of the process resumes.

For example:

  • A process receives an event from S/4HANA of a new employee/BP.
  • Some approval steps and other steps are executed.
  • Once approved, the process calls a Badge system to create an entry badge for the new person to get into the premises.
  • That Badge system does what it does.
  • When the Badge system is finished, it calls the API connected to the "Wait for an API Call" step, which resumes the process. 

P.S.: This is the idea behind our new SAP Build CodeJam.

 

How It Works

Add "Wait for an API Call" Step

Simply click the + sign to add a step, and go to Controls and Events area.

Then select the Wait for an API Call step.

You then give the trigger a name.

That's all you need to do.

If you want the API call that resumes the process to include additional data, you can define "input" parameters. The UI is the same as when specifying Process Inputs.

 

Create API Key

When calling the API for resuming the process instance, you must provide an API key with the proper permissions.

Go to the Control Tower, enter your environment, and select API Keys. 

Click Add API Key.

  1. Give the key a name.
  2. Give the key the following scopes:
    • trigger_read
    • trigger_execute

When calling the API, you must send the API key under a header called irpa-api-key.

 

Call the API 

Once you deploy the process to an environment, a separate trigger is created in that environment for that step -- in the same way an API trigger is created to trigger a new process instance.

Go into your environment, then to the Triggers tab. You'll see a trigger for your wait step.

Select the trigger and click View.

This will show you API details that are slightly different than the API details for the standard process API trigger.

The URL:

  • Standard API Trigger: A fixed URL (for each landscape). For EU10, for example, it is https://spa-api-gateway-bpi-eu-prod.cfapps.eu10.hana.ondemand.com/workflow/rest/v1/workflow-instances
  • Wait Trigger: The URL path is different and it includes a unique GUID that defines that specific trigger. If you create more than one Wait trigger in a process, they will have different GUIDs. I am not aware of a way to get this GUID at runtime.

The payloads are also different.

  • Standard API Trigger: Contains a definitionId (plus all the process inputs) so the system knows which process you want to trigger.
  • Wait Trigger: Contains an executionId (plus any inputs defined for the Wait trigger) so the system knows which process instance needs to be resumed.

That API can only be used if the specified process instance is actually waiting for that specific API call.

Remember to send your API key under the irpa-api-key header.

 

Getting the Process Instance ID 

You will need to send the process instance ID to the 3rd-party system that will resume the process instance, so they can include it in the request body of the API.

One way to do this is to create a custom variable to hold the instance ID.

Add Script Task to get the instance ID from the info object and put it in the custom variable.

 

Deleting Wait Triggers

A kind of quirky behavior occurs when you delete a "Wait" trigger and then redeploy – AND you have process instances still running from the previous version of your process.

👉If you delete the "Wait" trigger, what happens to those process instances?

Well, no worries. 

The old processes will continue to run and the old "Wait" trigger can still be used. It will continue to appear in the Control Tower.

When the last process that uses that trigger is completed, that "Wait" trigger will disappear from the Control Tower.

 

Errors

I have not re-created all the potential errors, but so far have figured out when the following status codes are returned when calling the "Wait" API.

  • 204: Success
  • 400: The GUID in the URL does not represent an existing "Wait" trigger, or you did not send an API key.
  • 403: The process instance ID passed in the request body does not exist or that process instance is not waiting for an API call.

This is just based on a few minutes testing, so it may not be 100% accurate. And there may be cases when the API returns other status codes. If you experience any, please let us all know.

entry.title: Navigating the Transition to SAP SuccessFactors: A Practical Guide for HR Leaders.
entry.content:
As HR leaders face increasing demands to modernize their organizations and align with strategic business goals, moving to a cloud-based HR solution like SAP SuccessFactors is becoming a critical step. But this transition is not without its challenges. In this article, we’ll explore key questions HR leaders often ask about implementing SAP SuccessFactors and how to approach the change with confidence and clarity.

How does SAP SuccessFactors fit into my overall technical architecture?

Currently, your HR functions are integrated into your ERP system. Transitioning to SAP SuccessFactors as a cloud solution represents a shift, but one designed to complement other SAP Line of Business (LoB) solutions such as finance, supply chain, sales, and other LoBs. SAP provides a set of productized integration points to ensure seamless data flow between systems while introducing additional capabilities unique to SAP SuccessFactors.

For example, while both your current SAP HCM (on-premise) and SAP SuccessFactors HCM cover core HR functionalities, the latter includes advanced tools like Career Development Planning (CDP) and Artificial Intelligence-driven features that extend beyond what HCM offers.

This comparison table highlights these differences and serves as a starting point for understanding how the new architecture can meet your business needs:

 

 

HR Capability*

SAP HCM Module

SAP SuccessFactors Module

Core HR Data Management

PA – Personnel Administration

EC – Employee Central

Organizational Structure Management

OM – Organizational Management

EC – Employee Central

Payroll Processing

PY – Payroll

ECP – Employee Central Payroll

Time Management

PT – Personnel Time Management

EC Time Tracking – Employee Central Time Tracking

Personnel Development

PD – Personnel Development

LMS – Learning Management System, CDP – Career Development Planning

Talent Management

TM – Talent Management

PMGM – Performance and Goals, Succession and Development

Learning and Training

LSO – Learning Solution

LMS – Learning Management System

Recruitment

EREC – E-Recruiting

RM – Recruiting Management, RMKT – Recruiting Marketing

Onboarding

N/A (SF Only)

OB – Onboarding

Performance Management

PA-PM – Performance Management

PMGM – Performance and Goals

Compensation Management

PA-CM – Compensation Management

CM – Compensation Management

Benefits Administration

BN – Benefits

EB – Employee Central Benefits

Workforce Reporting

BI – Business Intelligence

People Analytics – People Analytics

Workforce Analytics

WFA – Workforce Analytics

WFA – Workforce Analytics

Workforce Planning

WFP – Workforce Planning

WFP – Workforce Planning

Employee Self-Service

ESS – Employee Self-Service

ESS via EC – Employee Self-Service

Manager Self-Service

MSS – Manager Self-Service

MSS via EC – Manager Self-Service

Time Recording

CATS – Cross-Application Time Sheets

TAM – Time and Attendance Management (Custom Integration)

Travel and Expense Management

FI-TV – Travel Management

(Not an SF Modules) SAP Concur – Travel and Expense Management

Continuous Performance Management

N/A (SF Only)

Continuous Performance Management (CPM)

Employee Experience Management

N/A (SF Only)

Employee Central – Employee Experience Management (via Qualtrics)

Diversity and Inclusion Analytics

N/A (SF Only)

People Analytics – Diversity and Inclusion Insights

Opportunity Marketplace

N/A (SF Only)

OM – Opportunity Marketplace

Talent Intelligence Hub

N/A (SF Only)

TIH – Talent Intelligence Hub

Artificial Intelligence

N/A (SF Only)

RM – Recruiting Management, OM Opportunity Marketplace, TIH - Talent Intelligence Hub, …

* The capabilities in this overview are examples. The list is not comprehensive and is only meant to illustrate essential differences.

What are SAP’s recommendations for implementation and planning?

Transitioning to SAP SuccessFactors is not simply an update—it’s a chance to transform HR operations. To ensure a smooth transition, SAP recommends the following high-level steps:

  • Define Your Target Architecture: Work with your system integrator to map out the HR technical architecture, integration points, and any third-party solutions. Use an HR Business Capability Map to align stakeholders.
  • Review and Adapt Processes: SAP’s SuccessFactors Process Library (SSPL)*  provides a comprehensive template for reviewing and adapting HR processes to align with modern best practices
    (* To access the SSPL you require an S-User. Contact your SAP Customer Support if you need help creating such a user. In the meantime, here is an article from my colleague Marijn, on the subject).
  • Select Your Implementation Approach: Consider phased rollouts versus a “Big Bang” implementation. SAP’s Architecture Leading Practice (ALP)* documents offer guidance on migration strategies and localization (* you will also need an S-User for this resource).
  • Build Your Plan: Create a timeline, milestones, and deliverables that align with your HR calendar and resource availability.
  • Prioritize Change Management: Identify how roles and responsibilities will shift, and plan for skills development and communication strategies. We recommend appointing a dedicated change manager to complement project management efforts.

How do we balance SAP’s process recommendations with our unique industry needs?

Many organizations worry about adopting SAP’s standardized processes, fearing they might conflict with their unique culture or legal constraints. SAP offers a pragmatic approach:

  • Strategic Priorities First: Understand your organization’s priorities and how HR supports business goals. Use SAP’s Leading Practice Recommendations to explore how SuccessFactors can address industry shifts and operational challenges.
  • Use the Standard Process Library as a Starting Point: The SSPL provides a reference model maintained by SAP, covering tasks, roles, and data objects. Customers can review processes against this template and address conflicts before implementation begins.
  • Customization ONLY Where Necessary: While staying close to the SSPL is recommended for simplicity and cost efficiency, SAP provides tools like the Metadata Framework (MDF), third-party extensions, and the Business Technology Platform (BTP) for legal or business-critical customizations.

Organizational changes required for adoption?

Change management is a critical driver of success. Shifts in responsibilities, processes, and tools require careful planning and support. For example:

  • Change in Responsibilities: Certain tasks may shift from HR specialists to managers or employees, requiring training and role adjustments.
  • New Process Scopes: Processes like onboarding may extend to new employee populations, requiring clear communication and guidance.
  • Cultural Adaptation: Processes like performance reviews may shift from annual assessments to continuous development, changing the purpose and focus.

To address these changes, SAP recommends leveraging resources like the EmLA program, which provides free training materials and tools to enable your project team and services like the Transition Planning Service and Organizational Change Management.

What does success look like post-implementation?

Success is ultimately defined by your organization’s goals. For SAP, it’s measured by system adoption and customer satisfaction. For you, it might mean cost savings, improved process efficiency, or enhanced employee experiences.

SAP includes a value engineer in the transition planning service to align design and implementation efforts with measurable KPIs. This ensures an ROI calculation that serves as a baseline for evaluating the project’s success in the years to come.

Moving to SAP SuccessFactors is more than a technical upgrade—it’s an opportunity to modernize HR and align it with business priorities. By leveraging SAP’s tools, methodologies, and advisory services, HR leaders can navigate this transition with clarity and confidence, ensuring long-term success for their organizations.

Note to Readers:

This article is a compilation of common questions HR leaders ask when considering a transition to SAP SuccessFactors. The Q&A format was chosen to improve readability and make the information more accessible.

What about you? What challenges or questions do you have about modernizing your HR systems? We’d love to hear from you! Share your thoughts, and let’s continue the conversation.

Additional resources and links

  • Overview of the Transition planning service for SAP SuccessFactors HCM
  • Blog Article from my colleague Egle where she describes the HR Leading Practice Advisory Service

entry.title: SAP Document and Reporting Compliance: Send PDF with XML file via email to your business partners
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

The SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, outbound invoicing options for Brazil allows you to send DANFE (NF-e) and DACTE (CT-e) in PDF format to your business partners via email. For SAP S/4HANA and SAP ERP, you use the NF-e/CT-e Monitor (J1BNFE) transaction to display the distribution status of the XML and PDF files for each electronic fiscal document.

Take a look at the updates in our What's New session.

Instructions to generate and submit the PDF with the XML file:

1 - Follow the manual instructions in SAP Note 3532295 to customize the attached form template (template.xdp) using the Adobe LiveCycle Designer. The file is a sample that can be used to create the template for the DANFE (NF-e). The maximum template size is set to 2 MB; remember to resize or compress images used in the file.

Editing the sample file in Adobe LiveCycle Designer.

2 - After creating a new template as an XDP file in the Adobe LiveCycle Designer, use a tool to encode the file to Base64 and save it to your computer. Since an XDP file has an XML format, you can copy its source code using a simple text editor before encoding it.

The content of an XDP file opened in a text editor.

3 - You must use an HTTP client or requests via curl to update the template in the service. In the following instructions, an HTTP client is used to create the requests (GET, POST, PUT, and DELETE) and set the endpoint to the URL https://nfe.cfapps.br10.hana.ondemand.com. Refer to the SAP Business Accelerator Hub for further details.

3.1 – The following process relies on token authentication (also known as bearer authentication), which is used over HTTPS (SSL). This authentication allows you to access URLs of our service. The client must send the token in the authorization header when making requests to protected resources:

 

Authorization: Bearer <token>

 

For more details about token authentication in the SAP BTP, check the following pages:

  • OAuth JWT Bearer Authentication
  • OAuth 2.0 – Understanding Token Exchange

3.2 - In the HTTP client, create a POST request with the following details:

  • URL: https://nfe.cfapps.br10.hana.ondemand.com/pdf-service/v1/templates
  • Method: POST
  • Auth: Input the generated authentication token to communicate with our cloud services. Use your clientId and clientSecret to get the token in the SAP BTP.

3.3 – In the Body, you must send the content using the HTTP client, filling in the following details:

  • Template name in templateName field, which can be whatever you want.
  • Electronic document type in documentType field, which must be filled in with “NFE” for DANFE or “CTE” for DACTE.
  • Action type in actionType field, filled in with “AUTHORIZE”.
  • Country in country field, filled in with “BR”.
  • Electronic document layout in the version field; this is an optional field, and in the example, it is filled in with “4.00”, which is the current NF-e (DANFE) layout version.
  • Environment Type in environmentType field; this is an optional field, and it is filled in with “HOMOLOGATION” for testing purposes, or “PRODUCTION”.
  • The content of the XDP template, encoded to Base64, must be pasted into xdpTemplate field.

 

{
	"templateName": "Name of the template",
	"documentType": "NFE",
	"actionType": "AUTHORIZE",
	"country": "BR",
	"version": "4.00",
	"environmentType": "HOMOLOGATION",
	"xdpTemplate":
"Content of the template encoded to Base64 here"
}

 

Template update using an HTTP client.

Note: There is a restriction for documentType, actionType, country, version, and environmentType; you should not send two or more templates that have the same values in these fields.

4 - Open the following pages in SAP Help to understand how to update the email addresses of your business partners and confirm that jobs are scheduled in your backend system.

  • Configuring Email Addresses to Send XML and PDF Files
  • Scheduling Jobs for NF-e, CT-e, NFS-e

5 - Submit an NF-e document to your business partner in the NF-e/CT-e Monitor (J1BNFE) transaction. When the document is authorized by SEFAZ, the Distribution Log column will be updated.

6 - Click on the icon in the Distribution Log column to check the details of the email sent in Distribution History > Log. For more details, check the Distribution Bounce Statuses page in SAP Help.

Steps in the NF-e/CT-e Monitor (J1BNFE) transaction.

The email with the PDF and XML files will be received by your business partner.

Note: After updating the template, it may take up to 15 minutes for the new version to be used, after which your business partners will receive the new PDF version and XML file via email.

You can sign up for email alerts when new SAP Notes are released or updated in the component LOD-LH-NFE (Outbound invoicing option for Brazil); just follow the instructions in SAP Knowledge Base Article 2171560.

Use the regulatory change manager tool to find out if a solution to a legal change is planned. Take a look at SAP Knowledge Base Article 3421995 and the regulatory change manager page to understand how to use this tool.

Thank you, and until next time,
Thiago


Tags: brasil, einvoice, einvoicing, e-invoicing, electronic invoice, electronic invoicing, electronic document, electronic documents, eletronic document, edocument, edoc, e-document, e-docs, e-doc, e-docs, drc, document compliance, nota fiscal eletrônica, eletronica, electronic nota fiscal, factura electrónica, conhecimento de transporte eletrônico, nf-e, ct-e

entry.title: Building Dynamic Pop Ups using oDataV4 RAP Service in Fiori applications
entry.content:
INTRODUCTION

In modern Web Applications Dynamic Pop Up is a very common scenario.As a user we want to provide some details for an action to be executed on document. It could be a Comment or some reference number etc.

In this blog we are showing a scenario where depending on

Purchase Order Type the "Release " action requires different parameters.e.g. For a Standard PO (NB), only a comment is required, however for a Stock Transfer (STO) user needs to also provide a Reference number.

This is a simple requirement, however the solution we are going to cover in this blog is applicable for complex real world scenario as well.

Giving you a sneak peek into what we are going to achieve.

Prerequisite

1.Set up a Dev Space in Business Application Studio.

2.You have setup a user in SAP BTP ABAP environment.

3.You have downloaded and installed latest ABAP Development tools(ADT)

4. Basic understanding of SAPUI5 and RAP based Service.

Development Steps

STEP 1: Create Package ZPUR_ID# in you eclipse ADT environment.

STEP 2: Create Database Table ZPO_TBL

 

 

 

@EndUserText.label : 'Table for purchase order'
@AbapCatalog.enhancement.category : #NOT_EXTENSIBLE
@AbapCatalog.tableCategory : #TRANSPARENT
@AbapCatalog.deliveryClass : #A
@AbapCatalog.dataMaintenance : #RESTRICTED
define table zpo_tbl {

  key client            : abap.clnt not null;
  key po_num            : ebeln not null;
  org                   : ekorg;
  vendor                : lifnr;
  status                : abap.char(1);
  type                  : abap.char(2);
  reason                : abap.char(40);
  refno                 : abap.char(10);
  create_by             : abp_creation_user;
  created_date_time     : abp_creation_tstmpl;
  changed_date_time     : abp_locinst_lastchange_tstmpl;
  local_last_changed_by : abp_locinst_lastchange_user;

}

 

 

 

 

Step 3: Generate CDS Services ( Others objects ) using the wizard

This is a very useful feature. Press Next .

Click on Next and Finish.

Step 4: At this stage you can publish your service. 

Step 5 : Add a virtual Field

After Generation a new Virtual Field was added in CDS ZC_PO_TBL 

This is a boolean field and we are going to use it to manage visibility of parameters on action dialog.The logic for value of this field will be written in class ZCL_PO_CALC

Add below additional code to generated code for ZC_PO_TBL

 

 

  //Add New Virtual Element
  @ObjectModel.virtualElementCalculatedBy: 'ABAP:ZCL_PO_CALC'
  @EndUserText.label: 'Virtual Element'
  virtual HideAdditionalParams: abap_boolean,

 

 

Step 6: Create ABAP Class ZCL_PO_CALC

 

 

 

CLASS zcl_po_calc DEFINITION
  PUBLIC
  FINAL
  CREATE PUBLIC .

  PUBLIC SECTION.

    INTERFACES if_sadl_exit .
    INTERFACES if_sadl_exit_calc_element_read .
  PROTECTED SECTION.
  PRIVATE SECTION.
ENDCLASS.



CLASS zcl_po_calc IMPLEMENTATION.


  METHOD if_sadl_exit_calc_element_read~calculate.

    DATA: lt_calculated_data TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF zc_po_tbl.

    CHECK NOT it_original_data IS INITIAL.

    MOVE-CORRESPONDING it_original_data TO lt_calculated_data.

    LOOP AT lt_calculated_data ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<fs_data>).
      CASE <fs_data>-Type.
        WHEN 'NB'.
          <fs_data>-HideAdditionalParams = 'X'.
        WHEN 'ST'.
          <fs_data>-HideAdditionalParams = ''.
      ENDCASE.

    ENDLOOP.

    MOVE-CORRESPONDING lt_calculated_data TO ct_calculated_data.

  ENDMETHOD.


  METHOD if_sadl_exit_calc_element_read~get_calculation_info.
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

 

 

The logic applied here is that when type of Purchase Order is NB then the action should allow only Remarks parameter. In case it is of type ST then show additional parameters .Please note this is a basic logic which can be replaced by actual business requirement.

Step 7: Create an abstract entity ZA_PO_PARAMS

 

 

 

@EndUserText.label: 'Abstract CDS for Demo Actions'
define abstract entity ZA_PO_PARAMS
 // with parameters parameter_name : parameter_type
{
    
    .hidden:true
    .defaultValue: #( 'ELEMENT_OF_REFERENCED_ENTITY: HideAdditionalParams' )
    HideAdditionalParameters: abap_boolean;
    
    @EndUserText.label: 'Reference Number'
    .hidden: #(HideAdditionalParameters)
    ReferenceNumber: abap.char(10);
    
    @EndUserText.label: 'Remarks'
    .multiLineText : true
    Reason: abap.char(100);
    
}

 

 

Here we have added a field HideAdditionalParameters of type Boolean and getting it's value populated by the virtual field created earlier.ELEMENT_OF_REFERENCED_ENTITY is the keyword which allows access to values from the consumption CDS.This is a technical field and hence it is always hidden on UI.Next we have used this property to determine visibility of other property.

Step 8: Add Action in root entity Behaviour Definition ZR_PO_TBL

 

action Release parameter ZA_PO_PARAMS result [1] $self;

 

Step 9 : Refer to the action in Consumption Behaviour definition ZC_PO_TBL

 

use action Release;

 

 

Step 10 : Add UI annotations for actions. Go to Metadata Extension and open ZC_PO_TBL

 

.lineItem: [{ type: #FOR_ACTION, dataAction: 'Release', label: 'Release', importance: #HIGH, invocationGrouping: #CHANGE_SET }]

 

Following Objects were created.

Development Object TypeName
PackageZPUR_ID#
TableZPO_TBL
Root Entity CDSZR_PO_TBL
Consumption CDSZC_PO_TBL
Metadata ExtensionZC_PO_TBL
Behaviour Definition

ZC_PO_TBL

ZR_PO_TBL

Service DefinitionZUI_PO_TBL_O4
Service BindingZUI_PO_TBL_O4

Step 11 : These are our final steps to see the Fiori Application. Login to Business Application Studio.

Generate a Fiori application using Fiori Application Generation Wizard and selecting List Report from the available set of template. Select the oData Service we generated in previous steps as the data source.

Right click on your generated project in BAS and open page map.Click on add controller extension to your list report.We are adding controller extension as "HideAdditionalParams" being a technical field is not shown on the UI.At the same time we need to fetch it from backend for our logic to work.

Add highlighted code in generated extension controller file.

And we are done !!! You can preview the Fiori application.

Additional Tips: In case you face any issues , most common issue might be mismatch in some name. So review your code once. Sometimes just unpublishing and publishing the oData Service is helpful.

entry.title: How can SAP Build and SAP Integration Suite support your journey to SAP S/4HANA Cloud?
entry.content:
Yesterday, while presenting SAP’s financial results for FY2024, our CEO Christian Klein explained that we exceeded our cloud goals, accelerating cloud revenue and current cloud backlog growth against a much larger base. He also mentioned that half of our cloud order entry are now including AI. Clearly, our aim is to provide our customers with a suite of cloud-based applications that provides the most valuable data in business and powers unique AI capabilities. In this blog post, I would like to present how SAP Build and SAP Integration Suite – both running on SAP Business Technology Platform – are at the heart of SAP’s strategy and support our customers in their journey from legacy ERPs to SAP S/4HANA Cloud.


We know that for many SAP customers, the transition to SAP S/4HANA can be challenging, because someone in the organization has added a lot of custom code to your ERP 😉. That’s why SAP has created RISE with SAP, a packaged offering that helps you transform on-premise SAP ERP systems, modernize business processes, and become more agile in the cloud. And in RISE with SAP, you will find anything you need to fulfill your needs.

Let me be a bit more specific: every company in the world uses software to run their operations. We all know the most efficient and effective way is to use standard software like our SAP S/4HANA ERP. At the same time, we are fully aware that every organization in the world is unique and gains competitive advantage through their very specific processes. This might be the way they run processes for their customers because they offer unique experiences, or the way they engage with their suppliers because they may be faster in the supply chain processes, or it might be their HR processes which allows them to retain the best talent in the market… whatever it is, that makes your company unique, it should be reflected in your software because no one likes manual processes in 2025. In the context of migrating from SAP ECC to SAP S/4HANA Cloud, following the Clean Core strategy is a powerful opportunity: by eliminating custom code, technical debt, and standardizing the processes and data that underpin ERP operations, your business will benefit from more best practices while being able to customize what it needs to differentiate itself. And when it comes to standardization to achieve clean core with proper extensions and integrations, SAP Build and SAP Integration Suite on SAP BTP enter the game!

SAP Build and SAP Integration Suite add great value to your Cloud ERP

SAP Build allows you to extend and personalize SAP S/4HANA to meet your needs. It accelerates development and automation of extensions with low-code, pro-code, and generative AI tools. It enables stable, secure, and scalable ERP extensions that adhere to a prescribed extensibility model, with compliance to code quality standards and best practices. Tailored for every SAP customer, any size, any landscape, any industry, SAP Build is the best of suite tools for developers and key users collaborating in AI-powered ERP extension development.

Let me say it simply: SAP Build adds great value to your Cloud ERP. You will get more out of your cloud ERP investment with SAP Build. Just because it lets you easily customize, extend, integrate and automate business processes in your SAP S/4HANA Cloud solution to meet specific requirements while keeping the core clean. Watch our webinar “Extend SAP S/4HANA Cloud with SAP Build: your Free Toolkit”: in an hour, you will discover how you can leverage SAP Build’s low-code, pro-code, and generative AI development tools to drive innovation, automate processes, and supercharge your S/4HANA Cloud experience.

SAP Integration Suite is an iPaaS that helps you quickly integrate on-premises and cloud-based processes, services, applications, events, and data. For sure, it also adds value to your ERP: it connects business processes with proven pre-built integrations, APIs, connectors, and best practices. It provides upgrade-stable interfaces, proper monitoring and error resolution capabilities, and actively used and well-documented connectors.

For What Use Cases?

Let me give you very concrete examples where SAP Build is very helpful to address specific challenges in your Lead to Cash processes:

  • Digitize in-store cash sales processing
  • Automate the creation of sales orders from Excel files
  • Automate mass maintenance of billing blocks
  • Automate the approvals of return requests
  • Automate the creation of sales orders from unstructured data in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
  • Automate managing credit blocks on sales orders

and more…

I can also share many use cases that could improve your Source to Pay processes:

  • Centralize and streamline supplier communications with dedicated business sites
  • Mass maintenance of purchase contracts
  • Automate the creation of purchase requisitions from structured data in SAP S/4HANA
  • Automate the processing of paper-based invoices
  • Automatically populate missing material codes in purchase requisitions
  • Track sustainability data across your supply chain

and more…

You will find complementary use cases on our SAP Build web page.

And there is more: on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub, you can browse 500+ pre-built content packages for SAP Build and 600+ pre-built content packages for SAP Integration Suite. If you have developed custom code in your on-premises ERP in the past to address such use cases or specific needs, you can explore this vast catalog to see if some of these packages might be a good fit for your cloud ERP migration process. Indeed it is very important to know that SAP Build comes with pre-built content for ECC: you can use these SAP Build packages on ECC already to standardize some extensions, and then all the extensions developed on ECC with SAP Build will taken to S/4HANA during the migration.

Insights from Customers

What I presented above is not only theoretical: customers who have been through this SAP S/4HANA journey share valuable insights that can really help you. I have selected two few interesting stories, in different industries.

Sony Pictures

Let me start with Sony Pictures Networks: relying on the legacy system, SAP ECC, their highly customized and siloed infrastructure struggled to align with the company's integrated business processes. And decentralized data presented SAP challenges in consolidating business reports until the end.

Sony decided to remaster their operations for the future through next-generation ERP, with SAP S/4HANA Cloud, and Enterprise Automation, combining SAP Signavio, SAP Build And SAP Integration Suite. Indeed, SAP Signavio radically accelerates business process transformation, and I engage you to read this blog post explaining how customers on SAP ECC systems can prepare for an SAP S/4HANA transformation with SAP Signavio.

“By migrating to SAP S/4HANA and adopting SAP Business Technology Platform, we can keep the core clean. This strategy allows for faster development of any new technologies or any new process rollouts and enables us to take advantage of generative AI as part of our way forward” explains Mahesh Patil, SVP and Head of IT – Infrastructure, Integration, and Data Lake, Sony Pictures Networks India. And the results are excellent:

  • 100% automated process mining, reducing effort from weeks to days
  • 50% of interfaces managed through SAP Integration Suite
  • ~85% faster mobile app creation, reducing effort from four weeks to three days

Hitachi

Would you like another good example? Let’s take Hitachi High-Tech Corporation: they replaced their highly customized multinational business landscape by investing in SAP S/4HANA Cloud in a two-tier public and private cloud model. With a fit-to-standard ethos, the company can now focus on supporting business innovation across its subsidiaries using a side-by-side approach on SAP Business Technology Platform.

“Our new policy is to fit our business processes to standard as much as possible, reducing our need for customizations” says Yuri Yasuda, Manager Corporate DX Department, Digital Transformation Business Group, Hitachi High-Tech Corporation, who shares impressive KPIs:

  • 94% reduction in customization footprint
  • 18x faster handling of solution updates
  • 100% of on-site maintenance requirements eliminated

You can also find inspiration in the S/4HANA projects that Mahindra Group, PX Energy, LEOCH, Farys, Delta Light or Prestige have successfully implemented.

Start your S/4HANA journey with the support of SAP Build and SAP Integration Suite!

I hope that you’re now convinced that SAP BTP in general and SAP Build and SAP Integration Suite in particular are very powerful to ease your migration to SAP S/4HANA. To start this journey, watch our short video and request a customized demo: together, let’s make your business unstoppable!

entry.title: Launching the RISE with SAP Methodology Dashboard for Your SAP Transformation Journey
entry.content:
In today’s fast-paced business environment, transformation is essential. As customers embark on their journey, they require tools that provide clarity on their current position, the status of key projects, and insights that guide their next steps and strategy. This is where the RISE with SAP dashboard proves invaluable, helping organizations keep their ERP systems agile, innovative, and optimized for continuous improvement, unlocking SAP’s full potential.

What is the RISE with SAP Methodology Dashboard?

The RISE with SAP Methodology Dashboard is designed to support businesses in their move towards a clean core approach. This approach focuses on building resilient business processes and extensions supported by seamless integration, efficient operations, and data quality. It emphasizes the adoption of standard processes and the creation of extensions for differentiation, resulting in streamlined operations and increased efficiency.

Clean core is essential to help ensure customers’ business-critical systems remain agile, cost-effective, and ready to adopt innovations that drive business forward. The RISE with SAP Methodology dashboard is designed to support customers on their transformation journey to a clean core landscape.

Key Features and Benefits

The dashboard delivers a comprehensive set of capabilities to monitor and track the status of your systems, offering valuable insights and recommendations and ensuring alignment with SAP's best practices.

Designed for lead architects, the first release of the dashboard focused on clean core dimensions and provides the following features:

  • Access a detailed overview of system adherence and optimization opportunities for production or non-production S/4HANA, directly aligned with SAP's clean core approach
  • Analyze detailed insights and data on the clean core status of your systems, hosted on the SAP Cloud ALM (Application Lifecycle Management). SAP Cloud ALM is a key component of our integrated toolchain, offering a robust cloud environment optimized for daily updates and frequent functionality deployments.
  • Manage system extensibility with the dashboard, to identify deviations from SAP's recommendations and prepare for future upgrades. It supports maintaining a streamlined system with minimal customizations. This ensures that your systems are ready for future upgrades and that any necessary changes are well planned and executed.

                        System View to review your current extensions

 

We are committed to continuously enhancing its features and add new persona-based views, with regular updates that introduce new capabilities for clean core dimensions and transformation accelerators.

Next Steps: How to Get Started

The RISE with SAP is available to all customers with a RISE contract. Starting today customers can now access the RISE with SAP Methodology dashboard.

Want to learn more about how you can leverage this powerful tool for your SAP S/4HANA systems? Visit the SAP Help Portal to access detailed resources on the RISE with SAP Methodology dashboard. There, you’ll find step-by-step guides, FAQs, and best practices to help you get the most out of this innovative tool.

Let’s transform your SAP S/4HANA environment for success!
